p: A team project developed by a group of four people. The project includes a website, a series of social media videos, and a style guide, all created as part of a unified visual and digital solution.
The project was commissioned by a small private company looking to refresh its brand identity through a new website concept, improved functionality, and a cohesive visual identity.
The primary target audience is young people aged 17–25 who are preparing for their driving licence or are currently taking the mandatory first aid course.

description.head: Role
description.text: UI/UX Designer • Front-end Developer • Social Media Content Planner

description.head: Technologies
description.text: PHP • HTML • SCSS • Bootstrap • JavaScript • SQL • phpMyAdmin

p: To establish the project's visual direction, we began by creating a moodboard. Its primary purpose was to communicate a sense of trust, safety, and mutual support.

Once completed, the moodboard was tested with representatives of the target audience. Participants were asked to describe the emotions and associations it evoked. The most common responses included safety, community, trust, and mutual support, confirming that the chosen visual direction successfully reflected the project's intended values.

Based on the approved moodboard, we developed a style guide that brought together the project's core visual identity elements, including the color palette, typography, logo, and guidelines for their consistent use.
Based on the results of the moodboard testing, green was chosen as the primary color, as it best represented the desired atmosphere of trust and safety.
Red was used as an accent color for buttons and important interactive elements to draw users' attention to key actions.
Pastel colors were selected as a supporting palette. They help maintain the project's friendly atmosphere and enhance the gamification elements of the website.

The Roboto typeface was selected for the project due to its modern, minimalistic appearance and high readability. As one of the most commonly used typefaces in digital interfaces, it is already familiar to the younger audience through its use in Android and many popular applications.
Its simple and clear letterforms ensure comfortable reading of both educational materials and quizzes, supporting a smooth and user-friendly website experience.
The logo combines two simple symbols — a heart and a medical cross. Together, they create associations with care, trust, and first aid.
For the wordmark, the Myriad Pro typeface was chosen because it complements the rounded shapes of the symbol and creates a balanced visual identity.
The word Eksperten is highlighted in red to make the name more noticeable and improve brand recognition.
The prototype design was developed collaboratively by the entire team. We used one team member's initial concept as a foundation and further refined it together.
My contribution included designing specific interface elements, such as the purchase confirmation modal window, as well as improving and refining selected screens.
During the design process, we focused especially on gamification and simple user interactions to make the learning experience as clear, engaging, and accessible as possible.
As part of promoting the project, we created three short videos for social media platforms.
Instead of using a traditional serious approach, we incorporated humor and a short-form format to capture the attention of the younger audience and make the topic of first aid more approachable.
To demonstrate how the project could continue to grow after launch, we also developed a one-week content plan for social media.

When creating the plan, we focused on finding a balance between a consistent posting schedule and the time required to produce video content. For this reason, posts were scheduled for three days a week—Monday, Wednesday, and Friday. Each post was planned for 4:00 PM, a time commonly recommended for higher user engagement on social media.
The content plan was built around the videos created as part of the project. Their purpose was to maintain audience interest, increase brand awareness, and attract new users to the platform.
A team project developed by a group of four people. The website was created for a small private company that wanted to update its design and improve the user experience of its service.

The target audience is people with disabilities who need information about the accessibility of public places. The goal of the website is to help users find locations that meet their needs by providing detailed information about their accessibility.

description.head: Role
description.text: UI/UX Designer • Front-end Developer • Logo Designer

description.head: Technologies
description.text: HTML • SCSS • Bootstrap • JavaScript • JSON • SQL

p: To better understand the needs of the target audience, we conducted practical user research (User Journey).
For this research, we selected four local locations that people using wheelchairs might potentially visit. We created a route between these places and conducted two separate testing sessions.
During the first route, one of the team members used a wheelchair, allowing us to identify potential challenges and accessibility barriers from the user's perspective.

The second testing session was conducted together with a person who uses a wheelchair on a daily basis. During the route, we conducted an interview to understand what information is most valuable when searching for accessible locations.
The collected insights became the foundation for the website content regarding accessibility and influenced further design decisions.

alts: Map of the path that had to be covered during the research (User Journey)

p: To define the future visual direction of the project, we created a moodboard, which was later tested with representatives of the target audience.

During the testing, participants described their associations with the moodboard. The most common associations were trust, functionality, simplicity, and accessibility, confirming that the chosen visual direction matched the needs of the project.

Blue was chosen as the primary color because it is associated with trust, safety, and reliability. Additionally, this color is widely used in digital products, especially in financial services, where building user trust is important.
White is used for the background, while black is used for text to ensure high readability. Red, green, and yellow are used to indicate different levels of accessibility of locations.
To make the information understandable also for people with color vision deficiencies or visual impairments, accessibility icons are used alongside color-based indicators.
The DM Sans typeface was chosen for the project due to its simple, modern appearance, high readability, and good adaptation to digital interfaces.
Its clean forms and balanced structure ensure comfortable text perception and support the overall simplicity and accessibility of the design.
Since the main function of the website is to help users find accessible locations, the logo was based on elements commonly associated with map services.
The logo represents movement towards a destination and the search for an accessible route, directly reflecting the main idea behind the project.
The prototype design was created by the entire team. One team member’s concept was used as a starting point, after which we collaboratively improved and refined the design.
The main focus was placed on creating a simple and minimalistic design that would make the website accessible, understandable, and easy to use.

The prototype was tested together with a representative of the target audience. The testing focused on navigation, interface clarity, and overall usability. The collected feedback confirmed that the chosen design solutions met the needs of the users.
